verses.context: The domestic version of vairagya — not abandoning family but ceasing to over-identify with them as sources of identity and security.
verses.practical_application: Anabhishvanga (non-over-attachment) in relationships means engaging with full love while not making them the source of your identity. This same non-attachment applies to the outcomes of your sadhana — engage fully but do not over-identify.

verses.sanskrit:
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ||
verses.transliteration: mayi chananya-yogena bhaktir avyabhicharini | vivikta-desha-sevitvam aratir jana-samsadi
verses.hindi_meaning: मुझ परमेश्वर में अनन्य योग से अव्यभिचारिणी भक्ति, एकांत स्थान का सेवन और जन-सभा में अरति (अनासक्ति)।
verses.english_meaning: Unwavering devotion to Me through exclusive yoga, resort to solitary places, and absence of fondness for the company of people.
verses.context: The bhakti and solitude requirements for jnana — undivided devotion combined with preference for silence and seclusion.
verses.practical_application: Vivikta-desha-sevitvam (resorting to solitary places) is a practical prescription. The quality of your solitude — your meditation space, your time alone — directly affects the quality of your inner life. Protect it.

verses.sanskrit:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः |
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ||
verses.transliteration: anaditvat nirgunatvat paramatmayam avyayah | sharira-stho pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate
verses.hindi_meaning: हे कौन्तेय! अनादि होने से और निर्गुण होने से यह अव्यय परमात्मा शरीर में रहकर भी न कुछ करता है और न लिप्त होता है।
verses.english_meaning: Being without beginning and without qualities, O son of Kunti, this indestructible supreme Self — though dwelling in the body — neither acts nor is tainted.
verses.context: The key verse of Chapter 13: the Paramatman within the body is never actually affected by any of the body's actions or experiences.
verses.practical_application: Even in a moment of failure, the Atman within you is uncontaminated — na lipyate (not tainted). You are not your relapse. The witness-Self is always pure. Rise from this understanding, not from self-punishment.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव |
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ||
verses.transliteration: sri bhagavan uvacha | prakasham cha pravrittim cha moham eva cha pandava | na dveshti sampravrittani na nivrittani kankshhati
verses.hindi_meaning: श्रीभगवान बोले — हे पाण्डव! जो प्रकाश (सत्त्व), प्रवृत्ति (रजस्) और मोह (तमस्) के उत्पन्न होने पर उनसे द्वेष नहीं करता और निवृत्त होने पर उनकी आकांक्षा नहीं करता।
verses.english_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: O Pandava, one who does not hate illumination, activity, or delusion when they arise, nor craves for them when they cease.
verses.context: The gunatita (one beyond the three gunas) description begins: such a person neither hates the gunas when present nor craves them when absent.
verses.practical_application: On a day of clarity (sattva), do not become attached to that clarity. On a day of restlessness (rajas) or dullness (tamas), do not hate the state. Simply observe with the witness awareness — that is gunatita practice.

verses.sanskrit:
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते |
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ||
verses.transliteration: udasinavad asino gunair yo na vicalyate | guna vartanta ityeva yo vatishtati nengati
verses.hindi_meaning: जो उदासीन की भाँति बैठा रहता है, गुणों से विचलित नहीं होता, यह जानकर कि 'गुण ही परस्पर व्यवहार करते हैं' — वह अचल रहता है।
verses.english_meaning: One who remains seated like an unconcerned witness, not disturbed by the gunas, knowing that it is the gunas that are acting — that person stays firm and does not waver.
verses.context: The practical attitude of gunatita: 'the gunas are acting among themselves — I am the witness.' This understanding holds the key to non-identification.
verses.practical_application: When craving arises: 'rajas is acting in this body-mind — I am the witness.' When dullness: 'tamas is acting — I am the witness.' This is the gunatita posture that dissolves compulsive identification with the guna-driven impulse.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः |
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसञ्ज्ञैर्
गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ||
verses.transliteration: nirmana-moha jita-sanga-dosha adhyatma-nitya vinivrtta-kamah | dvandvair vimuktah sukha-duhkha-sanjnair gachchhanty amudhah padam avyayam tat
verses.hindi_meaning: जो मान और मोह से रहित हैं, आसक्ति-दोष से जीते हुए हैं, नित्य अध्यात्म में स्थित हैं, कामनाओं से विनिवृत्त हैं, सुख-दुःख नाम के द्वन्द्वों से मुक्त हैं — वे मूढ़ता-रहित लोग उस अव्यय पद को प्राप्त होते हैं।
verses.english_meaning: Free from pride and delusion, victorious over the evil of attachment, ever devoted to the Self, desires gone, free from pairs of opposites — the undeluded attain that imperishable state.
verses.context: The five qualities for attaining the imperishable state (Brahman): ego-free, attachment-conquered, Self-devoted, desire-free, beyond pairs of opposites.
verses.practical_application: Vinivrtta-kamah (desires completely turned back) is the advanced stage of brahmacharya — not suppressed desires but desires naturally withdrawn. This is built gradually through the five qualities listed. Each one supports the others.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च |
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ||
verses.transliteration: sarvasya chaham hridi sannivishto mattah smritir jnanam apohanam cha | vedaish cha sarvair aham eva vedyo vedanta-krit veda-vid eva chaham
verses.hindi_meaning: मैं सबके हृदय में अन्तर्यामी रूप से स्थित हूँ। मुझसे ही स्मृति, ज्ञान और उनका अपोहन होता है। मैं ही सभी वेदों से जानने योग्य हूँ, वेदान्त का कर्ता और वेदों का जानने वाला भी मैं ही हूँ।
verses.english_meaning: I am seated in the hearts of all; from Me are memory, knowledge, and their absence. I alone am to be known by all the Vedas; I am the author of Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas.
verses.context: God declares himself the inner source of all memory, knowledge, and its withdrawal — the inner teacher is God himself dwelling at the heart.
verses.practical_application: When insight arises in meditation, it comes from the divine seated within. When clarity is absent, that too is divine will. This understanding transforms both insight and dryness in practice into devotional experiences.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः |
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ||
verses.transliteration: sri bhagavan uvacha | abhayam sattva-samshuddhir jnana-yoga-vyavasthitih | danam damash cha yajnash cha svadhyayas tapa arjavam
verses.hindi_meaning: श्रीभगवान बोले — अभय (निर्भयता), सत्त्वसंशुद्धि (अन्तःकरण की शुद्धि), ज्ञानयोग में स्थिति, दान, दम (इन्द्रिय-निग्रह), यज्ञ, स्वाध्याय, तप और सरलता।
verses.english_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge, charity, self-control, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity, and uprightness.
verses.context: The daivi sampat (divine qualities) list begins — the opposite of the asura-nature that drives compulsive behaviour.
verses.practical_application: Dama (self-control of the senses) appears as the fifth divine quality. Brahmacharya is dama in action. Notice that it sits within a cluster of virtues — purity, austerity, study — that support and reinforce each other.

verses.sanskrit:
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् |
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ||
verses.transliteration: ahimsa satyam akrodhas tyagah shantir apaishunam | daya bhuteshv aloluptvam mardavam hrir achapalam
verses.hindi_meaning: अहिंसा, सत्य, क्रोधाभाव, त्याग, शान्ति, अपिशुनता (चुगली न करना), प्राणियों पर दया, अलोलुपता (लोभहीनता), मृदुता, लज्जा और चंचलताहीनता।
verses.english_meaning: Non-violence, truth, freedom from anger, renunciation, tranquility, absence of malice, compassion for all beings, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, and absence of restlessness.
verses.context: Continuation of the daivi sampat list — ahimsa, satya, akrodha at the head, alongside compassion and non-greed.
verses.practical_application: Aloluptva (freedom from greed/craving) is the direct anti-kama quality. Notice that non-violence (ahimsa), including non-violence toward oneself, is the first quality listed. Harsh self-judgment after failure is a form of himsa.

verses.sanskrit:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता |
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ||
verses.transliteration: tejah kshama dhritih shaucham adroho natimanita | bhavanti sampadam daivim abhijatasya bharata
verses.hindi_meaning: तेज, क्षमा, धृति (धैर्य), शुचिता, द्रोहाभाव और अतिमान का अभाव — हे भारत! ये दिव्य सम्पत्ति के लिए उत्पन्न पुरुष के लक्षण हैं।
verses.english_meaning: Vigour, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of malice, and absence of excessive pride — these belong to one born to the divine estate, O Bharata.
verses.context: The final verse of the daivi sampat list — closing with forgiveness and the absence of excessive pride.
verses.practical_application: Kshama (forgiveness) includes forgiving yourself after a fall. Dhritih (fortitude) means continuing without collapse. Together they describe the character that makes long-term sadhana possible: resilient and non-prideful.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः |
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ||
verses.transliteration: tri-vidham narakasyedam dvaram nashanam atmanah | kamah krodhas tatha lobhas tasmat etat trayam tyajet
verses.hindi_meaning: काम, क्रोध और लोभ — ये तीन नरक के द्वार हैं और आत्मा का नाश करने वाले हैं। इसलिए इन तीनों को त्यागना चाहिए।
verses.english_meaning: Lust, anger, and greed — these three are the triple gates to hell, destructive of the soul. Therefore one should abandon all three.
verses.context: The famous three gateways to narakam (hell, i.e. spiritual destruction): kama, krodha, lobha — desire, anger, greed.
verses.practical_application: The brahmacharya sadhana targets the first gateway directly: kama. But krodha (anger at perceived injustice, frustration when desires are blocked) and lobha (greed for more, better, longer pleasure) are equally active. Watch for all three.

verses.sanskrit:
एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः |
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ||
verses.transliteration: etair vimuktah kaunteya tamo-dvarais tribhir narah | acharaty atmanah shreyas tato yati param gatim
verses.hindi_meaning: हे कौन्तेय! इन तीन तमस के द्वारों से मुक्त पुरुष अपने कल्याण का आचरण करता है और फिर परम गति को प्राप्त होता है।
verses.english_meaning: O son of Kunti, a person liberated from these three gates of darkness practises what is good for the self and thereby attains the Supreme.
verses.context: The positive outcome: freed from the three gates, one automatically does what is beneficial and reaches the highest goal.
verses.practical_application: Liberation from kama, krodha, and lobha is not just the ending of suffering — it is the beginning of natural right action. The freed person 'acharati atmanah shreya' — acts for one's own highest good — spontaneously.

verses.sanskrit:
देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् |
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ||
verses.transliteration: deva-dvija-guru-prajna-pujanam shaucham arjavam | brahmacharyam ahimsa cha shariram tapa uchyate
verses.hindi_meaning: देवता, ब्राह्मण, गुरु और ज्ञानियों की पूजा, शौच, सरलता, ब्रह्मचर्य और अहिंसा — ये शरीर का तप कहलाते हैं।
verses.english_meaning: Worship of the gods, Brahmins, teachers, and the wise; purity, uprightness, brahmacharya, and non-violence — these are called the austerity of the body.
verses.context: The only verse that explicitly classifies brahmacharya as a sharirika tapa (austerity of the body) — giving it a specific place in the threefold tapas framework.
verses.practical_application: The Gita explicitly classifies brahmacharya as tapas sharirika — physical austerity at the level of the body. It is not punishment; it is disciplined development of the body as an instrument of the spirit. This is your sadhana's scriptural basis.

verses.sanskrit:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् |
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ||
verses.transliteration: anudvega-karam vakyam satyam priya-hitam cha yat | svadhyayabhyasanam chaiva van-mayam tapa uchyate
verses.hindi_meaning: जो वाणी अनुद्वेगकर (उद्वेग न देने वाली), सत्य, प्रिय, हितकर और स्वाध्याय के अभ्यास में तत्पर हो — वह वाणी का तप कहलाता है।
verses.english_meaning: Speech that is non-agitating, truthful, pleasant, beneficial, and engagement in regular scriptural study — this is called the austerity of speech.
verses.context: Tapas of speech: truthful, non-harming, kind, beneficial, and accompanied by svadhyaya (regular scriptural study).
verses.practical_application: Svadhyaya (study of scripture) is a component of speech-tapas. Reading your daily scripture on SadhanaOS is a tapas of speech and mind. Complement it by speaking only what is true, kind, necessary, and non-agitating.

verses.sanskrit:
मनःप्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः |
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ||
verses.transliteration: manah-prasadah saumyatvam maunam atma-vinigrahah | bhava-samshuddhir ity etat tapo manasam uchyate
verses.hindi_meaning: मन की प्रसन्नता, सौम्यभाव, मौन, आत्म-निग्रह और भाव की शुद्धि — यह मन का तप कहलाता है।
verses.english_meaning: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-restraint, and purity of motive — this is called the austerity of the mind.
verses.context: Mental tapas: manah-prasada (mental peace), saumyatva (gentleness of mind), mauna (inner silence), atma-nigraha (self-control), bhava-samshuddhi (purity of intention).
verses.practical_application: Bhava-samshuddhi (purity of motive) is the deepest tapas — doing sadhana for the right reasons. If practice is done for ego display or fear rather than genuine growth, it remains tamasic or rajasic. Purify the motive daily.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् |
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ||
verses.transliteration: yajna-dana-tapah-karma na tyajyam karyam eva tat | yajno danam tapash chaiva pavananyi manishinam
verses.hindi_meaning: यज्ञ, दान और तप-रूपी कर्म त्यागने योग्य नहीं है — वे अवश्य करने योग्य हैं। यज्ञ, दान और तप ही बुद्धिमान पुरुषों को पवित्र करने वाले हैं।
verses.english_meaning: Acts of sacrifice, charity, and austerity should not be abandoned — they are duty. Sacrifice, charity, and austerity purify even the wise.
verses.context: In the classification of what should not be abandoned: tapas (which includes brahmacharya per 17.14) must never be renounced.
verses.practical_application: Tapas purifies even the jnani — it is not just for beginners. No matter how advanced your understanding, the discipline of brahmacharya continues to purify the instrument through which that understanding is expressed. Never abandon the practice.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् |
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ||
verses.transliteration: yat tad agre visham iva pariname mritopamam | tat sukham sattvikam proktam atma-buddhi-prasadajam
verses.hindi_meaning: जो सुख पहले विष के समान लगे परन्तु परिणाम में अमृत के समान हो — वह आत्मा और बुद्धि की प्रसन्नता से उत्पन्न होने वाला सात्त्विक सुख कहलाता है।
verses.english_meaning: That happiness which is like poison at first but like nectar at the end — arising from the clarity of the Atman and intellect — is declared to be sattvic happiness.
verses.context: The three types of happiness: sattvic (difficult at first, nectarous in result), rajasic (sweet at first, bitter in result), tamasic (dull throughout).
verses.practical_application: Brahmacharya is the sattvic happiness of 18.37 — it is 'vishsm iva agre' (like poison at first) in its difficulty and discipline, but 'amrtopamam pariname' (like nectar in its fruit). Hold this truth when the practice is hard.

verses.sanskrit:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् |
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ||
verses.transliteration: vishayendriya-samyogad yat tad agre mritopamam | pariname visham iva tat sukham rajasam smritam
verses.hindi_meaning: जो सुख इन्द्रिय और विषय के संयोग से उत्पन्न होता है और पहले अमृत-समान लगे परन्तु परिणाम में विष-समान हो — वह राजसिक सुख कहलाता है।
verses.english_meaning: That happiness which arises from contact of senses with their objects, which is like nectar at first but poison in the end — that is declared to be rajasic.
verses.context: The exact inverse of sattvic happiness: sensory pleasure appears nectarous at first (amrtopamam agre) but is poison in its result (visham iva pariname).
verses.practical_application: This is the core insight for overcoming kama: the pleasure looks like nectar but is actually poison. Every moment of temptation is the agre-amrita facade. Behind it is the parinam-visha you know from experience. Remember the parinam.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः |
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ||
verses.transliteration: sve sve karmany abhiratah samsiddhim labhate narah | sva-karma-niratah siddhim yatha vindati tach chhrnau
verses.hindi_meaning: अपने-अपने कर्म में तत्पर मनुष्य संसिद्धि (पूर्णत्व) प्राप्त करता है। अपने कर्म में लगा हुआ मनुष्य जिस प्रकार सिद्धि पाता है — वह सुन।
verses.english_meaning: Engaged in one's own duty, a person attains perfection. How one engaged in one's own duty attains perfection — hear that.
verses.context: Each person attains samsiddhi (complete fulfillment) through their own svadharma — the path meant specifically for them.
verses.practical_application: Your brahmacharya sadhana is your svadharma right now. You don't need to compare your path with anyone else's. Your particular karma, your specific challenges, your unique sadhana — these are your direct route to samsiddhi.

verses.sanskrit:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया |
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ||
verses.transliteration: iti te jnanam akhyatam guhyad guhyataram maya | vimrishyaitad asheshena yathechchhasi tatha kuru
verses.hindi_meaning: इस प्रकार यह गुह्य से भी अधिक गुह्य ज्ञान मैंने तुझे बताया। इसे सम्पूर्ण रूप से विचार करके जो चाहे वह कर।
verses.english_meaning: Thus has knowledge more secret than all secrets been declared to you by Me. Reflect on it fully, and then do as you wish.
verses.context: God gives the teaching and then completely respects Arjuna's free will: 'reflect fully, and then act as you choose.' No compulsion.
verses.practical_application: Your practice is your choice. The Gita offers the knowledge; Krishna respects your freedom. The power of this path is that it asks for informed, willing choice — not compelled obedience. Choose it consciously, every day.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु |
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ||
verses.transliteration: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru | mam evaishyasi satyam te pratijane priyo si me
verses.hindi_meaning: मुझमें मन लगा, मेरा भक्त बन, मेरा पूजन कर, मुझे प्रणाम कर — सत्य कहता हूँ तू मुझे ही प्राप्त होगा। तू मुझे प्रिय है।
verses.english_meaning: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me, bow to Me. Truly I promise you shall come to Me, for you are dear to Me.
verses.context: One of the most intimate and direct promises in all scripture: 'you are dear to Me' — the divine addresses the individual soul with love.
verses.practical_application: 'Priyo asi me' — you are dear to Me. This is not abstract theology; it is a personal declaration. The divine loves you specifically. Carry this into your practice: you are not alone in this, and you are not unloved.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज |
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ||
verses.transliteration: sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam sharanam vraja | aham tvam sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shuchah
verses.hindi_meaning: सम्पूर्ण धर्मों को त्यागकर मेरी एक की ही शरण में आ। मैं तुझे समस्त पापों से मुक्त कर दूँगा — शोक मत कर।
verses.english_meaning: Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.
verses.context: The Charama Shloka — the final and most absolute instruction of the Gita: total surrender, total liberation, total reassurance.
verses.practical_application: When all techniques fail, when the mind is exhausted, when you feel lost in your practice — this verse remains. Surrender everything to the divine: your efforts, your failures, your fears. 'Aham tvam sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami' — I will free you from all sin. Do not grieve.
